title: Clearnote launches its AI-powered legal contracts platform - Music Ally
text:
Last October
we wrote about label Good Boy Records’ work
on a new service called
Clearnote
, which would use AI to draft music contracts for independent artists.
Now it has launched publicly, with its focus expanded to include film, TV and online creators too. On the music side, it can generate contracts for producer agreements, artist-label deals and collaborations among other tasks.
At launch, Clearnote is charging people $29.99 a month – which gives them three templates, 10 contracts and 300 credits to spend within the services. Custom plans are also available.
Good Boy Records VP of operations Cameron Siasi said that the private beta of Clearnote surprised the company with “how quickly the solution spread to fill the needs of other industries, including everything from more general businesses to independent creators and influencers in entertainment”.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
